events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: commercial electrical data/comm & security. Observed field condition: untwisted pairs at termination >½ in. Work staged. Need select correct tool/material/replacement approach and explain why observed choice is wrong. The observed condition is untwisted pairs at termination >1/2 inch. In structured cabling, TIA-568 specifies maximum untwist at termination (typically 0.5 in / 13 mm for Cat 5e/6). Untwisting more degrades performance (crosstalk, NEXT). Correct approach: use proper termination tool and technique, maintain pair twist up to termination point, trim to max 0.5 in untwist, use manufacturer-approved connectors, maybe use a punch-down tool with low pair separation. Since work staged, fail because non-compliant. Risk maybe medium? Data performance and inspection failure. Could be medium/high. Since not safety hazard but non-compliant and performance risk. Decision fail. Work_stage staged. percent_complete 0. component_conditions installed-defective? The work staged but observed condition on component? It says component condition categories may apply: installed-defective. Since materials staged, maybe not installed. But observed field condition is untwisted pairs at termination >1/2 in. Could be on patch panels/jacks already installed? Work staged means materials ready but not begun. Hmm. The condition could be observed on staged components. Use component_conditions ["non-compliant"] maybe also installed-defective? The schema says categories may apply. Since condition is defect in component/termination. Let's include "installed-defective" and "non-compliant". value 0. confidence 1.0. event_time 0. rate 0. order empty. workable empty. findings: "Untwisted pairs at termination exceed 1/2 in (TIA-568 limit)". actions: "Re-terminate with pairs twisted to within 1/2 in of termination point; use proper punch-down tool and connector; verify with cable certification test." rationale: TIA-568 limits pair untwist to 0.5 in to maintain impedance and crosstalk performance; NEC 800 requires listed communications cables and installations per manufacturer instructions. references: ["TIA-568", "NEC 800.110", "NEC 800.24"]. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Untwisted pairs at termination exceed 1/2 in, violating TIA-568 pair-twist limits.",
    "Excessive untwist degrades crosstalk/return-loss performance and will likely fail certification testing."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-terminate the cable with pair twist maintained to within 1/2 in of the termination point.",
    "Use the correct punch-down tool and manufacturer-approved connector/jack.",
    "Certify the link with a cable analyzer after re-termination."
  ],
  "rationale": "TIA-568 limits the amount of pair untwisting at the point of termination to about 1/2 inch to preserve the cable's electrical characteristics and minimize crosstalk. Allowing more than 1/2 inch of untwist is a non-compliant installation practice that can cause data-communication performance failures. NEC 800 requires communications cabling to be installed in accordance with listing and manufacturer instructions, which incorporate these standards.",
  "references": [
    "TIA-568",
    "NEC 800.24",
    "NEC 800.110"
  ]
}
